crystals.name: Hematite
crystals.subtitle: The Stone of Grounding
crystals.why: Its weight makes it a stone for planted, steady strength.
crystals.how_to_use: Hold it when you feel shaky, or carry it for steadiness.
crystals.desc: Heavy and cool in the hand, hematite is often reached for when strength needs a steady base. The weight itself is the point — a tactile cue to feel planted before pushing on.
crystals.care_note: Hematite can rust if left damp — keep it dry and wipe it with a soft cloth.

crystals.name: Obsidian
crystals.subtitle: The Stone of Truth

crystals.how_to_use: Hold it when you need to face what's hard, or keep it for backbone.
crystals.desc: A stone of truth and grounding, obsidian suits people whose strength comes from facing what's real. Honesty here is its own kind of weight, and the stone sits nearby as a quiet witness to it.

m1_quick: Red jasper, bloodstone, hematite, tiger eye, carnelian, pyrite, and obsidian are among the most commonly carried crystals for strength. Many people hold one as a cue to stay steady and keep going. Crystals are complementary wellness tools; real strength is built through rest, effort, and time, not granted by a stone.
m2_understanding: Strength is less about force than about staying power — the ability to keep going when the first burst of will has faded. Most hard things are marathons dressed as sprints. A small, tangible cue can mark the long middle: a stone held when the dip comes, a reminder that strength is built in small, steady choices more than dramatic ones.
m25_chose: We chose these seven because they speak to the most common strength intentions in crystal practice: endurance, vitality, planted steadiness, willpower, and strength through honesty. Their meanings come from crystal tradition and personal practice, not clinical evidence.
m5_use: Hold it when the dip comes. A stone in the palm when will flags.
Carry it through long efforts. A pocket stone as a reminder to stay steady.
Keep it on your desk. Where a glance prompts a steady push.
Pair it with rest. Strength is built between efforts, not only in them.
m6_combos: Pairing is about layering intentions. A popular combination is Red Jasper + Hematite — endurance paired with a planted base. Bloodstone + Tiger Eye is another, chosen for toughness and the will to hold firm.
m8_intro: Explore genuine natural crystal pieces chosen for staying power — stones, bracelets, and forms you can hold or carry, spanning the seven crystals above.
closing: Strength is staying power more than force — and a real stone can be a steady cue to keep going. Start with one and let small, steady efforts add up. For more, explore Personal Empowerment.

faq.a: Not directly. Crystals can't grant strength. Many people use them as tangible cues to keep going; real strength is built through rest, effort, and time, not a stone.

faq.q: Are crystals scientifically proven to increase strength?
faq.a: No. There is no clinical evidence that crystals increase physical or inner strength. Any felt benefit usually comes from the ritual of pausing and steadying, not a proven effect of the stone.

faq.q: Which crystal is best for strength and endurance?
faq.a: Red jasper is the most commonly chosen — long tied to stamina and steady staying power.

faq.q: What is the best crystal for toughness through a hard stretch?
faq.a: Bloodstone and hematite — bloodstone for vitality and backbone, hematite for a planted, steady base.

faq.q: Which crystal helps me hold my ground when I feel shaky?
faq.a: Tiger eye and pyrite — tiger eye for the will to stay firm, pyrite for steady, deliberate strength.

faq.q: How should I cleanse strength crystals?
faq.a: Smoke (sage, palo santo), sound (singing bowl), or moonlight work for most stones. Note that hematite can rust if left damp — keep it dry and wipe with a soft cloth; pyrite can tarnish if damp — wipe it dry.

faq.q: What is the best crystal bracelet for strength?
faq.a: A red jasper or tiger eye bracelet is most common — both durable and easy to wear daily as a cue to stay steady.
